varieties.name: Glen Large (Glenlarge)
varieties.origin: Gatton Research Station, Queensland, Australia (1990s). Developed specifically for subtropical Queensland climates.
varieties.traits: Large, day-length neutral, heat-tolerant. White to slightly purple-tinged bulb wrapper. Hard stem but does not produce true scapes.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, excellent bulb formation in subtropical conditions

varieties.name: Southern Glen
varieties.origin: Department of Primary Industries Queensland, Australia (2000s)
varieties.traits: Similar to Glen Large but slightly smaller, day-length neutral, excellent humidity tolerance
varieties.availability: Queensland DPI, specialty garlic suppliers, some Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Italian Pink
varieties.origin: Developed by Gleeson family, Swan Hill, Victoria, Australia (1970s-1980s). Possibly derived from Taiwanese Purple.
varieties.traits: Pink-skinned bulbs, hard neck that's easily removed, day-length neutral, heat-tolerant

varieties.name: Italian Red
varieties.origin: Australian selection, possibly from Taiwanese/Formosan stock (1970s-1990s)
varieties.traits: Reddish-purple coloring on bulb and cloves, subtropical adaptation, day-length neutral
varieties.yield_notes: Uniform, very large bulbs in subtropical conditions
varieties.availability: Australian specialty garlic suppliers, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: California Early
varieties.origin: California, USA (early 1900s). Widely grown commercial variety.
varieties.traits: Early maturing softneck, white wrappers, heat-adapted, non-bolting
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, Australian importers

varieties.name: Inchelium Red
varieties.origin: Colville Indian Reservation, Washington State, USA (pre-1900s). Heirloom variety from Native American tradition.
varieties.traits: Award-winning softneck artichoke, white bulbs with pink blush, heat-tolerant, adaptable

varieties.name: Italian Late
varieties.traits: Late-maturing softneck, white bulbs, heat-tolerant, productive

varieties.name: Lorz Italian
varieties.traits: Softneck artichoke with spicy, bold flavor. Heat-loving variety, well-adapted to hot summers
varieties.yield_notes: Strong yields in warm winter areas
varieties.availability: Heirloom suppliers, specialty garlic farms

varieties.name: Thermadrone
varieties.traits: Buttery French softneck, minimal cold requirement, early to mid-season maturity
varieties.availability: Specialty garlic suppliers, Grey Duck Garlic, Filaree Farm

varieties.name: Italian White
varieties.traits: Artichoke type softneck, creamy white bulbs and cloves, sometimes with purple tips

varieties.name: Italian Purple
varieties.traits: Attractive purple blush on skin, classic Italian flavor
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, Italian seed companies

varieties.name: Sicilian Gold
varieties.traits: Italian softneck with nice white skin, purple clove wrappers
varieties.availability: Boundary Garlic (Canada), specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Asian Tempest
varieties.traits: Asiatic weakly-bolting type with gorgeous dark purple cloves, early maturing, heat-tolerant
varieties.availability: Specialty garlic suppliers, Keene Garlic

varieties.name: Korean Mountain
varieties.traits: Asiatic type with strong tendency to develop 'neck bulbils', vigorous
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, Beets Workin Farm

varieties.name: Korean Red
varieties.traits: Asiatic type with bold, spicy flavor, early to mid-season
varieties.availability: Asian seed suppliers, specialty garlic farms

varieties.name: Pyongyang
varieties.traits: Late to emerge, matures mid-season, Asiatic type

varieties.name: Tzan
varieties.traits: Turban type with unique vegetable undertone, not traditionally garlicky, early maturing
varieties.availability: Grey Duck Garlic, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: China Dawn
varieties.traits: Turban type, early maturing, heat-tolerant
varieties.availability: Specialty garlic suppliers

varieties.name: Shandong
varieties.traits: Turban type, early season, warm climate adapted

varieties.name: Creole Red
varieties.origin: Spain via Caribbean to Americas (1500s-1700s). Heirloom variety.
varieties.traits: Striking rose-colored skin, small but attractive bulbs, heat and drought tolerant
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, heirloom collections

varieties.name: Ajo Rojo
varieties.traits: Creole type with beautiful red-purple coloring, heat and drought tolerant

varieties.name: Burgundy
varieties.traits: Creole type with burgundy-purple coloring, heat tolerant

varieties.name: Siberian
varieties.traits: Marbled purple stripe with exceptional HEAT tolerance for a hardneck, delicately flavored
varieties.yield_notes: Large bulbs even in extreme heat
varieties.availability: Grey Duck Garlic, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Bogatyr
varieties.traits: Marbled purple stripe with heat tolerance, deep true garlic flavor
varieties.availability: Grey Duck Garlic, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Metechi
varieties.traits: Marbled purple stripe with rich flavor, adapts to various climates

varieties.name: Nootka Rose
varieties.origin: San Juan Islands, Washington State, USA (heirloom)
varieties.traits: Silverskin type with silvery white skins, strong flavor, late maturing
varieties.availability: Heirloom suppliers, Pacific Northwest seed companies

varieties.name: Elephant Garlic (Allium ampeloprasum)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region (ancient). Actually a type of leek, not true garlic.
varieties.traits: HUGE bulbs (softball-sized), mild flavor, heat and humidity tolerant, easy to grow
varieties.availability: Widely available from nurseries, seed companies, garden centers

varieties.name: Yamuna Safed-2 (G-2)
varieties.origin: National Horticultural Research Development Foundation (NHRDF), Nashik, India
varieties.traits: White bulbs, heat-adapted, developed for Indian conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 40-100 quintals per hectare depending on conditions
varieties.availability: NHRDF Nashik, Indian agricultural universities, Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: Yamuna Safed-3 (G-50)
varieties.traits: Bigger cloves than G-2, popular for export, heat-tolerant
varieties.availability: NHRDF Nashik, major Indian seed suppliers, Kerala agricultural departments

varieties.name: Yamuna Safed-4 (G-282)
varieties.traits: Improved variety with better disease resistance, white bulbs
varieties.yield_notes: High yields with good bulb formation
varieties.availability: NHRDF, Indian agricultural research stations

varieties.name: Yamuna Safed-5
varieties.traits: Latest in Yamuna Safed series, improved characteristics

varieties.name: Yamuna Safed-8
varieties.traits: Recent release with enhanced qualities

varieties.name: Agrifound White (G-41)
varieties.traits: Pure white bulbs, good storage, developed for Indian conditions

varieties.name: Agrifound Parvati
varieties.traits: Bigger cloves, export quality, heat-adapted
varieties.availability: NHRDF Nashik, major seed companies in India

varieties.name: Agrifound Parvati-2
varieties.traits: Enhanced characteristics over Parvati-1, larger cloves
varieties.availability: NHRDF, progressive agricultural suppliers in Kerala

varieties.name: Ooty 1 🌱
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), Coimbatore, India. Clonal selection from local Ooty varieties.
varieties.traits: Indigenous to South India, adapted to tropical conditions, good storage
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields in South Indian conditions

varieties.name: Ooty 2 🌱
varieties.traits: Improved version of Ooty 1, indigenous South Indian variety

varieties.name: Punjab Garlic 🌱
varieties.traits: Notable for elevated S-allyl-cysteine (SAC) content (1.68 mg/g fresh weight), white bulbs
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields in North Indian conditions
varieties.availability: Punjab agricultural universities, North Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Rohtak Garlic 🌱
varieties.traits: Superior nutritional capabilities, highest phenolic content in methanol extract (4.03 mg GAE/g dry weight)
varieties.availability: Haryana agricultural research stations, specialty Indian suppliers

varieties.name: Tiruchirappalli (Trichy) Garlic 🌱
varieties.traits: Highest bulb weight among Indian varieties (25.33g average), indigenous South Indian

varieties.name: Creole Red
varieties.traits: Heat and drought tolerant, striking rose-purple color
varieties.availability: International heirloom suppliers, specialty garlic farms

varieties.name: Thermadrone
varieties.traits: Minimal cold requirement, specifically for warm climates, buttery flavor
varieties.availability: International specialty suppliers, Grey Duck Garlic

varieties.name: Lorz Italian
varieties.traits: Heat-loving, bold spicy flavor, loves hot summers
varieties.availability: Heirloom suppliers, international seed companies

varieties.name: Asian Tempest
varieties.traits: Asiatic type with dark purple cloves, early maturing, heat-tolerant

varieties.name: Korean Red
varieties.traits: Bold, spicy flavor, Asiatic type, warm climate preference

varieties.name: Inchelium Red
varieties.traits: Award-winning, highly adaptable, heat-tolerant softneck

varieties.name: Elephant Garlic (Allium ampeloprasum)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region (ancient). Actually a leek relative, not true garlic.
varieties.traits: Massive bulbs, mild flavor, EXCELLENT heat and humidity tolerance, easy to grow
varieties.availability: Widely available internationally, many seed companies

varieties.name: California Early
varieties.traits: Early maturing, heat-adapted, bred for California climate
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, US garlic farms

varieties.name: Sicilian Gold
varieties.traits: White skin with purple clove wrappers, mild to moderate

varieties.name: Italian Late
varieties.traits: Late maturing, heat-tolerant, dependable storage

varieties.name: China Dawn
varieties.traits: Very early maturing turban, heat-tolerant
varieties.availability: Asian seed suppliers, specialty garlic farms

varieties.name: Shandong
varieties.traits: Turban type, early season, warm climate adapted

varieties.name: Ajo Rojo
varieties.traits: Beautiful red-purple Creole, heat and drought tolerant

varieties.name: Burgundy
varieties.traits: Deep burgundy-purple Creole, heat tolerant

varieties.name: Rose de Lautrec
varieties.traits: Pink-purple Creole, heat-adapted, prestigious French variety
varieties.availability: French seed companies (limited), specialty suppliers
